commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair is queued because the remaining blocker is maintainer acceptance of the operator-facing status advisory and proof depth, not a narrow code defect.; Cleared: The diff is TypeScript diagnostic/test code and a shared matcher extraction; it does not change workflows, lockfiles, package scripts, dependency sources, permissions, or secret handling. (sha=ae2c02d64b9ca22d8747c6cd4250a32d24ae812d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds gateway status plugin-version drift detection for active official external installs, status rendering, tests, and shared official install-record matching used by update paths.

PR surface: Source +256, Tests +502. Total +758 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: yes. there is a high-confidence source/proof path: current main has no status drift diagnostic, and the PR body plus tests cover the detector, running gateway version, daemon env, activation policy, and render behavior. I did not run a full live two-process reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is queued because the remaining blocker is maintainer acceptance of the operator-facing status advisory and proof depth, not a narrow code defect.

target.title: feat(status): detect externalized plugin version drift in --deep
target.body:
## Summary

Surface **externalized npm/ClawHub plugins whose installed version doesn't match the running gateway** in `openclaw [gateway] status --deep`. Pure diagnostic, deep-only, read-only — no behavior change anywhere else.

## Motivation

`openclaw update` already syncs plugins via `updatePluginsAfterCoreUpdate`. But `npm install -g openclaw@<v>` bumps the gateway binary only — the postinstall script intentionally avoids touching plugin packages:

> `scripts/postinstall-bundled-plugins.mjs`: *"Plugin package dependencies are installed only by explicit plugin install/update flows, never postinstall."*

That's a deliberate design choice (postinstall is hot, plugin installs need network and can be slow). The unfortunate consequence: a gateway updated via `npm install -g openclaw@<v>` continues running silently mismatched against its externalized plugins until someone separately runs `openclaw plugins update <id>`.

In practice this just bit a real-world user (myself) on the **2026.5.3 → 2026.5.4** transition: gateway moved to 2026.5.4, `@openclaw/whatsapp` stayed on 2026.5.3 in `~/.openclaw-<profile>/npm/node_modules/` for ~6 minutes before I happened to notice it via `openclaw plugins list`. Nothing in `gateway status` or `channels status` flagged it; both reported healthy/connected. Easy class of latent bug.

This PR closes that gap by surfacing the drift in `status --deep` — the same surface that already reports gateway event-loop health, channel transport state, port issues, etc. Adding "plugin X is on 2026.5.3 but gateway is on 2026.5.4" fits the existing shape.

## Behavior

`openclaw gateway status --deep` (or `openclaw status --deep`) now includes a new section when drift is detected:

```
Plugin version drift: 1 externalized plugin not on gateway 2026.5.4
- whatsapp: 2026.5.3 (npm) → expected 2026.5.4
Fix: openclaw plugins update <plugin-id> for each drifted plugin, then openclaw gateway restart.
```

When all plugins match, no extra output (silent). When run without `--deep`, no overhead at all. Bundled plugins (`origin: "bundled"`) ship with the gateway and are explicitly skipped — they cannot drift. Disabled plugins (`plugins.entries.<id>.enabled: false`) are also skipped since their version is not load-bearing.

## What this PR does **not** do

- ❌ No auto-update of plugins (deliberate; mirrors existing UX where channel/port issues are surfaced but not auto-resolved)
- ❌ No new CLI subcommand
- ❌ No change to the non-deep status path
- ❌ No new dependencies

## Files

- **`src/plugins/plugin-version-drift.ts`** (new, 120 LOC): pure `detectPluginVersionDrift()` helper. Compares each enabled externalized (`npm` / `clawhub`) install record against the gateway `VERSION`. Build-suffix qualifiers (`-1`) are normalized identically on both sides so `2026.5.4-1 ≈ 2026.5.4`.
- **`src/plugins/plugin-version-drift.test.ts`** (new, 172 LOC): 10 unit tests covering:
  - match / drift / suffix normalization
  - ClawHub source coverage
  - ignored sources (archive/path/git — those are operator-pinned local artifacts)
  - missing-version skip (cannot fabricate a comparison)
  - disabled-in-config skip
  - default-enabled-by-absence
  - deterministic alphabetical sort
- **`src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.ts`**: populate a new optional `pluginVersionDrift` field on `DaemonStatus`, only when called with `deep: true`. Failure to read install records is non-fatal — status renders normally.
- **`src/cli/daemon-cli/status.print.ts`**: render the new section when drifts exist, with a fix hint pointing at the existing `openclaw plugins update` command.
- **`src/cli/daemon-cli/probe.ts`**: (no net change after rebase) the daemon-cli probe wrapper already surfaces the running gateway `server`/`version` from the handshake; drift now consumes it.

_No `CHANGELOG.md` change — release-note context lives in this PR body per the repo's release-owned changelog policy._

## Verification

- ✅ `tsgo --noEmit -p tsconfig.core.json` — no errors in any touched file
- ✅ Pre-commit hooks pass
- ✅ `vitest run` on `plugin-version-drift.test.ts`, `status.gather.test.ts`, `probe.test.ts` — **49/49 pass** (includes 4 new gather regression tests)
- ✅ Rebased clean onto current `main`

## Review response (revision after ClawSweeper review)

Addressed all three findings:

- **[P2] Compare drift against the running Gateway version** — drift now uses the running gateway version resolved from the probe handshake (`rpc.server?.version` / `rpc.version`), falling back to the invoking CLI `VERSION` only when no gateway version is available. This removes the false-positive where a newer CLI probing an older still-running gateway flagged plugins that actually match the gateway.
- **[P2] Read install records from the daemon service environment** — `loadInstalledPluginIndexInstallRecords({ env: mergedDaemonEnv })` now reads the managed service's profile/state dir instead of the invoking CLI process's, so a CLI running under a different profile no longer produces missed/false drift.
- **[P3] Remove the CHANGELOG.md edit** — done; the branch no longer touches `CHANGELOG.md`.
- **Dirty branch** — rebased onto current `main`; conflicts resolved (the obsolete schema-snapshot workaround commit was dropped as it's no longer needed against current main).
- Added focused `status.gather` regression tests for the running-version and profile-mismatch cases.

## Why I think this belongs in core (not as a ClawHub skill)

This is filling a gap in an existing core diagnostic surface, not introducing new behavior. The same `status --deep` already reports gateway event-loop degraded states, channel transport issues, port conflicts, and stale gateway PIDs — adding plugin version drift fits that exact pattern. A ClawHub skill could implement the same detection externally, but the natural home for "is everything OK with my gateway?" is `openclaw status --deep`, where users already look.

Happy to adjust scope, naming, or output format based on review.



## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Externalized npm/ClawHub plugins whose installed version doesn't match the **running gateway** weren't surfaced anywhere. After review, drift is computed against the running gateway version (from the probe handshake), not the invoking CLI `VERSION`, and install records are read from the managed daemon service's profile/state dir. The detector still skips version-matched, explicitly-disabled, and non-externalized (path/bundled) plugins.

**Real environment tested:** macOS (Darwin arm64, Mac Studio), Node v25.9.0, pnpm 11.2.2. Branch `feat/plugin-version-drift-status-deep` rebased onto current `main` (head `e6074c9`), `pnpm install` completed. The real `detectPluginVersionDrift` module from `src/plugins/plugin-version-drift.ts` was imported and executed via `tsx` (no mocks), driven by a realistic install index. The CLI/Gateway version split and the service-env install-record read are additionally covered by real `status.gather` tests that drive `gatherDaemonStatus` with a mocked probe returning `server.version` and a daemon service env distinct from the CLI process env.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```
$ git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD
feat/plugin-version-drift-status-deep
$ git log --oneline -2
e6074c9 fix(status): compute plugin drift from running gateway + service env
3358da8 feat(status): detect externalized plugin version drift in --deep
$ npx tsx proof-harness.mts                          # real detection module, bug-vs-fix
$ npx vitest run src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts \
                 src/cli/daemon-cli/probe.test.ts \
                 src/plugins/plugin-version-drift.test.ts
```

Scenario: invoking CLI is **2026.5.6**, the still-running gateway is **2026.5.4**. Install index: `@openclaw/whatsapp`=2026.5.3 (npm, behind running gateway), `@openclaw/discord`=2026.5.4 (npm, matches running gateway), `@openclaw/signal`=2026.5.2 (npm, but disabled in config), `context-topics` source `path`.

**Evidence after fix:** Live stdout copied from the run against the real module:

```
=== OLD behavior (gatewayVersion = CLI VERSION) — the bug ===
Against CLI 2026.5.6, discord(2026.5.4) is WRONGLY flagged as drift: [ '@openclaw/discord', '@openclaw/whatsapp' ]

=== NEW behavior (gatewayVersion = running gateway from probe) ===
{
  "gatewayVersion": "2026.5.4",
  "drifts": [
    {
      "pluginId": "@openclaw/whatsapp",
      "installedVersion": "2026.5.3",
      "gatewayVersion": "2026.5.4",
      "source": "npm",
      "packageName": "@openclaw/whatsapp"
    }
  ]
}

=== Rendered `openclaw status --deep` drift section (fixed) ===
Plugin version drift: 1 externalized plugin not on gateway 2026.5.4
- @openclaw/whatsapp: 2026.5.3 (npm) → expected 2026.5.4
Fix: openclaw plugins update <plugin-id> for each drifted plugin, then openclaw gateway restart.

=== Assertions ===
running-gateway version used:                   true
whatsapp reported (behind running gateway):     true
discord NOT reported (matches running gateway): true
signal NOT reported (disabled):                 true
context-topics NOT reported (path):             true
no false positive vs CLI version:               true

(test suite)
 ✓ src/plugins/plugin-version-drift.test.ts (10 tests)
 ✓ src/cli/daemon-cli/probe.test.ts (11 tests)
 ✓ src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts (28 tests)
 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  49 passed (49)
```

**Observed result after fix:** Under the old logic (CLI `VERSION` = 2026.5.6) `@openclaw/discord` was **wrongly** flagged as drifted even though it matches the running gateway. After the fix (running gateway version = 2026.5.4), only the genuinely-behind `@openclaw/whatsapp` is reported; `@openclaw/discord` (matches running gateway), `@openclaw/signal` (disabled), and `context-topics` (non-externalized `path`) are all correctly omitted — no false positives. The `status --deep` drift section renders verbatim in the expected format. The full suite (drift + probe + gather, including the 4 new regression tests exercising the running-version and service-env paths) passes 49/49.

**What was not tested:** End-to-end invocation of the live `openclaw gateway status --deep` CLI binary against a physically version-mismatched on-disk plugin install under a separate running gateway service. The harness drives the real detection module directly, and the gather-level wiring (running gateway version from the probe + service-env install-record read) is covered by real `status.gather` tests rather than a live two-process CLI run.
target.author: hussein1362
